title: Explain Like I'm Five
description: Turn any complex topic into a simple, vivid explanation for any audience.
body:
Explain {{topic}} in simple terms that a {{audience}} could understand.

Use one short, concrete analogy from everyday life, avoid jargon, and keep it under 150 words. End with a single sentence on why it matters.

title: Cold Outreach Email
description: Draft a short, personable cold email that actually earns a reply.

variables.name: valueProp
variables.label: Core value proposition
variables.placeholder: e.g. cut onboarding time in half

title: Code Review Buddy
description: Get a focused review of a snippet: bugs, readability, and one concrete fix.
body:
Review the following {{language}} code:

{{code}}

Do three things, in order:
1. Call out any bugs or correctness issues.
2. Note the single biggest readability or maintainability improvement.
3. Show a revised version of the most important part.

Be specific and skip generic advice.

title: Debug This Error
description: Get a likely root cause and a targeted fix for a stack trace or error message.

variables.name: context
variables.label: Relevant code or context
variables.placeholder: Paste the function or config that's involved

title: Explain This Codebase
description: Get oriented in unfamiliar code: what it does, how it's wired, and where to start.

variables.name: code
variables.label: Code to explain
variables.placeholder: Paste a file, class, or module

title: Write Unit Tests
description: Generate focused tests for a function, including the edge cases you'd forget.

title: Build Me a Study Plan
description: Turn a learning goal and a time budget into a realistic week-by-week plan.
body:
I want to learn {{subject}} and I can spend about {{hoursPerWeek}} hours a week. My current level is: {{currentLevel}}.

Build me a realistic week-by-week plan that gets me to a working competence. For each week, give the focus, one concrete thing to build or practice, and how I'll know I've got it. Keep it honest about what won't fit, and front-load the highest-leverage fundamentals.

variables.name: currentLevel
variables.label: Current level
variables.placeholder: e.g. total beginner, comfortable with spreadsheets

title: Socratic Tutor
description: Learn a concept by being questioned through it instead of just being told.
body:
Be my Socratic tutor for {{concept}}. My level is {{level}}.

Don't lecture. Ask me one focused question at a time that moves me toward understanding, wait for my answer, then build on it. Gently correct my misconceptions when they show up. Start now with your first question.

title: Follow-Up After No Reply
description: Send a polite, non-needy nudge that gives the prospect an easy reason to respond.
body:
I emailed {{recipientName}} about {{topic}} {{timeAgo}} ago and haven't heard back.

Write a short follow-up that doesn't guilt-trip them. Add one new, genuinely useful angle or piece of value rather than just "bumping this." Keep it under 80 words and make replying feel easy. Then suggest the single best day/time to send it.

title: Handle the Objection
description: Get a calm, honest response to a sales objection that doesn't sound defensive.
body:
A prospect said: "{{objection}}"

My product is {{product}} and the real value is {{valueProp}}.

Give me a response that (1) acknowledges their concern as legitimate, (2) reframes it around the outcome they actually care about, and (3) ends with a question that moves the conversation forward. Avoid hype and don't be defensive. Give me one short version and one longer version.

variables.name: objection
variables.label: Their objection
variables.placeholder: e.g. it's too expensive for us right now

variables.name: valueProp
variables.label: Core value
variables.placeholder: e.g. saves each rep 4 hours a week

title: Rewrite This, Clearer
description: Tighten any draft into plain, confident prose without losing your meaning.

title: Blog Post Outline
description: Turn a topic and an audience into a structured outline you can actually write from.
body:
I want to write a blog post about {{topic}} for {{audience}}. The one takeaway I want them to leave with is: {{takeaway}}.

Give me a working outline: a headline option (plus one alternate), a one-sentence hook, 4-6 section headers with a line each on what they cover, and a closing that reinforces the takeaway. Mark the one section that will be the hardest to write well so I know where to spend my time.

variables.name: takeaway
variables.label: Main takeaway
variables.placeholder: e.g. you don't need a CS degree to start

title: Summarize + Pull Action Items
description: Condense notes, a thread, or a transcript into a summary and a clear to-do list.

variables.name: content
variables.label: Notes / thread / transcript
variables.placeholder: Paste the meeting notes or message thread

title: Think Through a Decision
description: Pressure-test a choice with tradeoffs, risks, and a recommendation you can argue with.
body:
Help me think through a decision. The choice is: {{decision}}. What matters most to me here is {{priorities}}, and my constraints are {{constraints}}.

Lay out the realistic options, the key tradeoffs of each, and the biggest risk I might be underweighting. Then give me a clear recommendation and the single strongest argument against it, so I can stress-test my own thinking.

variables.name: decision
variables.label: The decision
variables.placeholder: e.g. take a new job or stay for the runway

variables.name: priorities
variables.label: What matters most
variables.placeholder: e.g. long-term growth and a healthy culture

title: Plan My Week
description: Turn a messy task dump into a focused, realistic plan for the week.
body:
Here's everything on my plate this week:

{{tasks}}

My most important goal is {{topGoal}} and I realistically have {{capacity}} of focused time.

Help me plan. Pick the few things that actually move my top goal, suggest what to drop or defer, and lay out a simple day-by-day shape for the week. Be honest if I'm trying to fit more than will fit.

variables.name: tasks
variables.label: Everything on your plate
variables.placeholder: Dump your tasks, one per line
